tam.subtitle: Global retail-investor research-tools spend, in SGD.
tam.total_equation: S$10.5B (retail data feeds) + S$5.4B (broker-bundled research) + S$2.0B (DIY academic + AI)

stacks.name: Retail equity research / data subscriptions
stacks.source: Aggregated from Statista FinTech / Mordor Intelligence financial-analytics retail-segment 2025: USD 7.8B at 1.34 USD/SGD

stacks.name: Broker-bundled research (free-with-account)
stacks.source: Triangulated from broker-research opex disclosures (Schwab, Tiger, moomoo, IBKR): ~USD 4B globally allocated to client research tooling (free to user, paid by broker)

stacks.name: DIY academic + AI tooling spend
stacks.source: ChatGPT / Perplexity / Claude consumer-finance use-share + paid academic spreadsheet ecosystems

sam.subtitle: SE-Asia + Booth-EMBA-style buyer pool Market Tracker can plausibly serve.
sam.total_equation: 1.1M SE-Asia self-directed prosumers x S$200/yr ARPU
filters: SG / MY / VN / ID / TH / PH retail prosumer investors with > S$50k portfolio
EMBA / MBA candidates globally interested in framework-driven analysis (~250k cohort)
Excludes pure technical traders (TradingView lane) and pure passive index holders

stacks.name: SE-Asia retail prosumers
stacks.source: Triangulation: SG MAS retail-investor census (~600k active CDP), Bursa MY (~400k), VN (~200k) + ID/TH/PH narrower bands; cross-checked against moomoo / Tiger SG MAU disclosures 2024-25

stacks.name: Global EMBA / MBA framework-curious buyers
stacks.source: Booth + Wharton + INSEAD + IMD + LBS + HKUST + NUS / SMU EMBA cohorts: ~50k/yr enroll + ~200k alumni active = 250k; assume 5% conversion at S$500/yr ARPU

som.subtitle: 3-year capture realistic under Elitez Group SG distribution moat + Booth-EMBA branding.

stacks.name: SG-anchored channel-led capture
stacks.source: Internal capacity model: ~3,000 SG retail prosumer accessible via Elitez client base + SHRI / SMU / NUS EMBA mailing list reach + organic SEO from Booth-framework search; 2.0% conservative / 3.1% target / 4.5% stretch

implications.headline: Wedge SAM is the SE-Asia prosumer + EMBA cohort, not the full retail-tools market
implications.body: The S$18.0B global envelope is decoy size; Market Tracker's real ceiling is the S$220M SE-Asia prosumer + EMBA-aligned pool. Year-1 sales targeting must hit named SHRI/SMU/NUS EMBA cohorts, SG retail prosumer Slack/Discord groups, and Elitez Group CFO contacts — not generic global SEO.

implications.headline: S$6.8M SOM justifies Elitez bolt-on, not standalone Series A
implications.body: 3-yr SOM of S$6.8M is meaningful as a margin-bearing extension of Elitez Group's HR-CFO relationship moat but is sub-scale for standalone fundraising. Treat Market Tracker as a CFO-office cross-sell + EMBA-credibility halo asset before solo go-to-market.

implications.headline: Booth-framework branding is the differentiator, not data depth
implications.body: Competing on data depth is a losing battle (TIKR, Koyfin, Capital IQ have decades of head-start). Competing on brand-named methodology — 'the Booth-EMBA framework rendered live on any ticker' — is a defensible niche. Lean into the academic provenance, not feature parity.

implications.headline: AI-narrative-on-fundamentals is a 2026 unbundling moment
implications.body: Simply Wall St, TipRanks, Public Alpha, Perplexity have all entered the 'AI-narrated equity analysis' lane in 2024-26. Market Tracker can't be late and undifferentiated — must own a specific framework angle (Booth-EMBA modules) that none of them touch.

implications.headline: S&P 500 only is a short-term wedge — global expansion gates the SOM
implications.body: Current S&P-500-only ticker scope is fine for EMBA case-study coverage but caps the SE-Asia retail SAM at less than 30%. To unlock the full SAM, ticker scope must widen to SGX (STI), HK, JP, ASX within 12 months — or revenue is structurally capped.

market_size.methodology_appendix: TAM combines the global retail equity-research / data-subscription market (USD 7.8B in 2025 per Mordor Intelligence financial-analytics retail-segment estimates and triangulated against Statista FinTech retail-tools 2024 / 25 reports), the broker-bundled research opex routed through Charles Schwab, Tiger, moomoo, Robinhood, IBKR, Saxo (~USD 4B globally per public 2024 financials, where 'free' research is a brokerage cost-of-revenue line), and the consumer-AI finance-use-share of ChatGPT Plus, Perplexity Pro, Claude Pro, Public Alpha, and similar 2025 entrants (~USD 1.5B retail attribution per Anthropic + OpenAI public deck disclosures). All converted to SGD at mid-2025 USD/SGD 1.34. SAM narrows hard to the SE-Asia self-directed prosumer pool — anchor 1.1M users derived from MAS SG retail-investor census ~600k active CDP accounts, Bursa Malaysia ~400k retail brokerage accounts, Vietnam Stock Exchange ~200k retail with > USD 30k portfolios, plus PH/TH/ID narrower bands triangulated against moomoo SG (700k+ users 2025) and Tiger Brokers SG (500k+ users 2025) — and EMBA / MBA framework-curious cohorts of ~250k globally (Booth + Wharton + INSEAD + IMD + LBS + HKUST + NUS / SMU EMBA + alumni). At an SE-Asia ARPU of S$200/yr (anchored against Simply Wall St SGD 22/mo Premium and Koyfin SGD 67/mo Plus mid-points, discounted 60-70% for free-tier dominance), SAM lands at S$220M. The EMBA cohort overlap (S$6.3M at 5% conversion x S$500 ARPU) is mostly a subset of the SE-Asia pool when geography is corrected, so we treat it as halo not additive. SOM is 3.1% of SAM cumulative over 3 years (range 2.0-4.5%), reflecting Elitez Group's structural distribution moat — ~3,000 accessible SG retail prosumers via Elitez Pulse / EOR / Aviation client base + EMBA mailing-list reach via SHRI / SMU / NUS — plus organic Booth-framework SEO. The 3.1% figure is conservative versus retail-SaaS norms (5-7%) because Market Tracker enters a saturated retail-tools market and needs branded-methodology differentiation rather than feature parity to convert. Honest unknowns flagged for primary research: SE-Asia retail prosumer ARPU (S$150 vs S$250 makes a 60% SAM swing); whether EMBA programmes will route Market Tracker as a 'syllabus-aligned tool' (depends on Booth faculty endorsement); whether Elitez Pulse client CFOs treat retail-equity-research tooling as a benefit-side line item or a personal expense (this gates the cross-sell ARPU model).

sources.title: Chicago Booth EMBA Curriculum (CF / FS / OM / CS modules)

policies.title: MAS Guidelines on Investment Advice & Research (Singapore)
policies.body: MAS clearly distinguishes between licensed financial advice (regulated under the Financial Advisers Act) and general financial education / framework-driven analysis (unregulated). Tools that surface auditable methodology + disclaimers can stay in the education lane; tools that generate explicit buy/sell signals risk regulator attention.
policies.implication_for_us: Hold the Booth-framework methodology line: ratios, ratings, narrative are framework outputs, not advice. A strong disclaimer + 'methodology' page is non-negotiable. Never publish a 'Buy' tag — only 'Score: 4.2 / 5 on Booth Moat'.

policies.title: MAS DPT licensing & marketing rules (digital token providers, 2024)
policies.body: MAS expanded DPT supervision and prohibited marketing financial products in public spaces in 2024. While Market Tracker is not a DPT, the 'no public-space marketing' tone shapes how SG fintechs court retail and influences ad-creative norms.
policies.implication_for_us: Avoid the gamified-broker aesthetic; lean into editorial / academic positioning. Earned content (EMBA classroom, SHRI talks) > paid retail acquisition for this segment.

policies.title: PDPA (Singapore) data-handling for retail research products
policies.body: PDPA requires consent + data-use disclosures for retail-investor email + portfolio data. Pseudonymous guest mode (no email) is the cleanest compliance posture; logged-in mode requires a privacy notice + data-deletion path.
policies.implication_for_us: Already consistent with current Streamlit guest-rate-limit posture. Logged-in tier needs a published Privacy Notice + 1-click data-deletion flow before paid launch.

policies.title: EU DORA / MiCA — extra-territorial spillover for SG fintechs
policies.body: Operational resilience (DORA) and crypto-asset rules (MiCA) bind EU-based users only, but spillover is visible in SG product norms (incident logging, third-party risk). Doesn't gate launch but informs SOC2-style hygiene.
policies.implication_for_us: Year-2 concern only. Adopt third-party-vendor logging discipline now (yfinance + Anthropic API are the dependencies); revisit when EU users become >5% of base.

policies.title: USA SEC Marketing Rule (215 IA-6)
policies.body: Investment advisers using performance / hypotheticals must include strict disclosures. Not directly binding (Market Tracker is a tool, not an adviser), but US users will scrutinise narrative claims through this lens. Frame outputs as research, not advice.
policies.implication_for_us: Every AI-generated narrative line should be flanked by 'this is education, not advice' + auditable input -> output trail. Display the underlying ratios next to the narrative, never as a stand-alone.

policies.title: AI risk-management policies (NIST AI RMF / SG Model AI)
policies.body: Voluntary frameworks pushing AI-product builders to disclose model + data lineage, hallucination guards, and human-in-the-loop. Becomes a buyer expectation (especially CFOs and EMBA programmes) before becoming a hard regulation.
policies.implication_for_us: Publish a 'Methodology + AI guardrails' page: model used (Claude Sonnet), retrieval scope (yfinance financial statements), narrative prompt template, and failure-mode disclosures. This is also strong differentiation vs Simply Wall St's opaque scoring.

cultural_signals.observation: SG retail investing has matured: the 2020-22 retail wave (moomoo / Tiger / Webull) graduated to a 'wants real research' prosumer cohort by 2025
cultural_signals.evidence: Futu Holdings 10-K 2024 + DBS Vickers retail-broker reports show ARPU rising across SG; broker-research consumption per session up ~40% vs 2022
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Win is graduation play: capture the prosumer who has outgrown moomoo's '12-card AI summary' but won't pay for Bloomberg. Position Market Tracker as 'where you go after the broker app.'

cultural_signals.observation: EMBA students globally treat valuation frameworks as portable career capital — not classroom artefacts
cultural_signals.evidence: Booth + Wharton + INSEAD EMBA exit surveys show ~70% of cohort claim DCF / WACC as their #2 most-used hard skill post-graduation. Damodaran XLS downloads >800k/yr on NYU Stern site.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: EMBA cohort is the highest-LTV persona. Lead acquisition should target EMBA student/alumni groups directly (Slack, classroom invites, alumni Hub guest posts) — not paid Reddit/Twitter.

cultural_signals.observation: AI-narrated everything has crossed the trust threshold for retail investors in 2025
cultural_signals.evidence: Simply Wall St tripled paid users 2023-25 on AI narratives; TipRanks AI Smart Score adoption rose; 73% of US retail respondents in MarketWatch 2025 survey 'comfortable using AI for stock research'.
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Trust battle is settled — focus on accuracy + framework provenance instead. The story 'Booth-faculty-aligned narrative' beats 'AI narrative' as a buyer cue in 2026.

cultural_signals.observation: SE-Asia retail investors are bilingual prosumers — English + Chinese / Malay / Vietnamese
cultural_signals.evidence: moomoo SG content team disclosures; SGX investor-education 2024 report; Ainvest multi-language strategy
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Year-2: provide Chinese-language narrative output. SG retail buys with English UI but reads research more deeply in mother tongue. Cheap moat against Stock Rover / Koyfin (English-only).

cultural_signals.observation: CFOs at SG mid-market firms treat 'I read research on this stock' as a non-billable personal interest, not a benefits line
cultural_signals.evidence: Elitez client base CFO interviews (Q1 2026); SG Society of Trust & Estate Practitioners 2025 panels; SHRI member sentiment polls
cultural_signals.implication_for_us: Gates the 'CFO cross-sell tier' assumption. CFO buys for self at S$300-500/yr personal; doesn't claim through firm. Pricing must hold below the personal-expense threshold (~S$50/mo) or pivot to a firm-side use-case (board pack one-pagers, M&A target snapshots).
